name: Ascent to La Maroma – Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park
name_original: Ascensión a La Maroma — Parque natural Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama
region: National Park Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama
description:
La Maroma is the highest peak in Malaga, so it is not surprising that it gives you some of the most incredible views in the region. From its summit you can see the Mediterranean Sea and even the high peaks of the Sierra Nevada.

The path is a constant climb that begins protected by pine trees and ascends to a place where there is less and less vegetation. Halfway there you find the Temple viewpoint, the perfect place to regain strength and enjoy the landscape. You do the last stretch cresting, in an exercise to maintain your balance and ignore the vertigo. Special mention to the horses and goats that can be seen many days right at the summit.

As for the weather, it is best to avoid summer due to the heat. In winter, you may encounter ice and some snow. The Route begins in the El Robledal recreational area, where you can park your vehicle. The place is also a controlled camping area that has barbecues, services and a fountain. In addition, just five kilometers away, you will find the Los Caños de la Alcaicería Hotel Restaurant, which offers you accommodation and a hearty dish to recharge your batteries.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around National Park Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama traverse a landscape characterized by rugged, diverse terrain, forming a natural border between Málaga and Granada. The park features dramatic topography with sharp peaks, deep valleys, and awe-inspiring ravines, predominantly composed of limestone and dolomitic marbles. Fast-flowing rivers carve through the area, creating waterfalls, while pine forests, holm oak, and various shrub species cover the slopes. Best easy hiking trails around National Park Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama The most popular easy hiking route is Hawk Hill – Huerta Grande Pass loop from Cómpeta, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 29 minutes to complete, offering views of the surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hiking loop from Troncos Petrificados, an easy 2.8 miles (4.5 km) path. This route explores areas known for unique natural features, including petrified tree trunks. Local hikers also love the El Saltillo Suspension Bridge – El Saltillo Suspension Bridge loop from Canillas de Aceituno, a 2.4 miles (3.9 km) trail leading through dramatic gorges and featuring a notable suspension bridge, often completed in about 1 hour 27 minutes. Hiking around National Park Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama is defined by rugged terrain, limestone cliffs, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, with many routes suitable for easy exploration. The routes in National Park Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,400 reviews. More than 6,000 hikers have used komoot to explore National Park Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in National Park Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama?
A: There are 24 easy hiking trails in National Park Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama. These routes are designed for accessibility, allowing hikers to explore the region's natural beauty without significant challenge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes in Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama?
A: Easy hikes in the park typically traverse rugged, diverse terrain characterized by limestone and dolomitic marbles, giving the mountains a distinctive grey and white appearance. You'll find paths winding through pine forests, holm oak groves, and areas with unique geological features like ravines and gorges. While easy, some routes may still offer gentle ascents and descents.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hikes in this region?
A: The National Park Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama is enjoyable for easy hikes throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, but routes along rivers like the Río Chillar provide opportunities for cooler walks. Winter offers crisp air and unique views, though higher elevations might experience colder conditions.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or rivers to see on easy trails?
A: Yes, the park is known for its fast-flowing rivers that carve through the landscape, creating spectacular features. The Río Chillar is particularly famous for its river walks, and you might encounter waterfalls like "Los Árboles Petrificados" on some routes. While not directly on an easy trail, the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1084101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Chillar riverbed&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural attraction in the area.

Q: What interesting natural features or landmarks can I find on easy hikes?
A: On easy hikes, you can discover various natural features. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38260371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Troncos Petrificados&lt;/a&gt; explores areas known for unique petrified tree trunks. The region's karstic landscape also means you might encounter impressive rock formations and views of deep valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7461955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hawk Hill&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic vistas.

Q: Are there any circular easy routes available?
A: Yes, many easy routes in the park are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38271639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hawk Hill – Huerta Grande Pass loop from Cómpeta&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21836268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of El Fuerte – View of the Sierra de Almijara loop from Frigiliana&lt;/a&gt;, both popular choices for their accessibility and scenic views.

Q: Are there family-friendly easy hiking options?
A: Absolutely. The easy trails are generally well-suited for families. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19351195" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Acebuchal village – El Acebuchal Village loop from Acebuchal&lt;/a&gt; offer manageable distances and gentle elevation changes, making them ideal for hikers of all ages. The diverse flora and fauna also provide engaging points of interest for children.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the easy hiking trails?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails within the National Park Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Be mindful of local regulations, especially in protected areas or near livestock.

Q: What are the typical durations for easy hikes in the park?
A: Easy hikes in the park typically range from 1 to 2 hours to complete. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38271639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hawk Hill – Huerta Grande Pass loop from Cómpeta&lt;/a&gt; takes about 1 hour 29 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21834304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Suspension Bridge – El Saltillo Suspension Bridge loop from Canillas de Aceituno&lt;/a&gt; is often completed in about 1 hour 27 minutes. These durations are estimates and can vary based on your pace and stops.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads for easy routes?
A: Yes, many trailheads for easy routes are accessible by car, and parking is generally available in or near the charming white villages that serve as gateways to the park, such as Cómpeta or Canillas de Aceituno. It's advisable to check specific route details for precise parking information.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the easy trails in National Park Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama?
A: The easy trails in National Park Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1,400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning panoramic views, the unique geological formations, and the opportunity to explore the region's rich biodiversity. The accessibility and varied terrain suitable for all skill levels are also commonly highlighted.

Q: Are there any charming villages or places to explore near the easy trails?
A: Yes, the park is dotted with charming white-washed villages, known as 'Pueblos Blancos,' which make excellent starting points or post-hike exploration spots. Villages like Cómpeta, Frigiliana, and Canillas de Aceituno offer a glimpse into the region's rich Moorish past and provide amenities like cafes and restaurants. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19351195" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Acebuchal village loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores one of these picturesque settlements.
tips: The El Robledal recreational area has stone tables, barbecues, water and a large area of pine forests and parking.
Along the path to La Maroma you will reach the Temple viewpoint from where you can enjoy splendid views.

This 2069 meter peak was originally called Pico Tejeda because it was the highest point in the mountain range of the same name. The name La Maroma comes from the ropes used to lower the snow that was stored in a natural chasm located 40 meters south of the trig point. 

This chasm was known as Sima de la Maroma and that led to the summit being named the same way. 

It is located between the provinces of Granada and Malaga, being the highest point of the second, and on clear days you can see the crescent-shaped coast drawn by the Alboran Sea from Ceuta to Oran.
